sub_mechanisms: M_1: pack K and V channel groups of 4 into a 4D hypercomplex vector
M_2: lattice quantize the 4D vector to the nearest integer point of the densest 4D lattice
M_3: decode via fast 4D-algebra multiplication during attention
M_4: per-block reconstruction error bound via lattice covering radius
stripped_llm_application: KV-cache compression that packs 4-channel groups into a 4D hypercomplex algebra; stored values quantized to the densest 4D integer lattice; attention reads decode via fast 4D-algebra multiplication; reconstruction error bounded by the lattice covering radius per block.
novelty_claim_stripped: Existing KV compression methods use real/complex transform coding (FAEDKV Fourier, CommVQ RoPE-VQ), spectral denoising (eOptShrinkQ), or sparse eviction. None use a 4D hypercomplex algebra packed on a closed-form densest 4D lattice with a covering-radius reconstruction bound.
